# 0001 — Popular Questions (คำถามยอดนิยม): hybrid single-table, auto-generated + admin-curated

Status: Accepted — 2026-07-11

## Context

The public portal and chat page showed a static, hardcoded `suggestedQuestions`
array (frontend `mockData.ts`) under the heading **"คำถามยอดนิยม"**. We wanted it
to be (a) **backend-backed**, (b) **admin-manageable**, and (c) **auto-generated**
from real usage — while still showing meaningful questions on a **fresh deploy**
that exercise the real external agencies live on prod (dopa / dol / fda, per
`spec/agent-20260623.md`).

These goals pull against each other: pure auto-generation has a cold-start problem
(empty on a fresh DB) and no curation; a pure admin list ignores "auto-generated";
and messy raw user text is not portal-appropriate.

## Decision

A **hybrid** feature backed by a **single `PopularQuestion` table**. Each row has a
`source` (`seed` / `auto` / `manual`), plus `pinned` / `hidden` / `sort_order` /
`score` and a normalized, **unique `text_key`**.

- **Generation** is an LLM synthesis pass (new `LlmRoute` purpose
  `popular_questions`, falling back to the classification model), not trigram
  frequency — because the portal needs clean, clickable Thai prompts and the LLM
  can tag each question with its agency in the same call. Runs on a 24h scheduler
  job over a 30-day window of successful user turns.
- **Cold-start gate**: below `POPULAR_QUESTIONS_MIN_TURNS` (default 20) successful
  turns, the job **no-ops** so the six seeded dopa/dol/fda questions remain.
- **Churn policy**: each run deletes only the previous **auto** rows that are
  unpinned **and** not hidden; `seed` / `manual` / `pinned` rows are never touched.
  Editing an `auto` row's text **flips it to `manual`** (locking it from churn).
- **Hidden = tombstone**: hidden rows persist, and any regenerated candidate whose
  `text_key` already exists (including hidden rows) is skipped — so a question an
  admin hid never resurrects.
- **Serving**: `GET /api/v1/public/popular-questions` is **anonymous** and returns
  the published set (not-hidden, pinned-first then score then recency, capped at
  `POPULAR_QUESTIONS_DISPLAY_COUNT`=8). Because the shared frontend `apiClient`
  attaches the JWT on every request, the global role chokepoint
  (`enforce_role_allowlist`) now **exempts any GET under `/api/v1/public/`** for all
  roles; otherwise authenticated `user`/`viewer` callers on the chat page get 403.
- **Agency resolution is lenient**: an unmatched agency name → `agency=null`
  (neutral icon), never invented, never dropped.
- **Display-only wiring**: seed questions are *authored for* dopa/dol/fda but we do
  **not** bake the prod endpoints or the `secret_key_555` credential from the spec
  into the repo — pointing agencies at live endpoints is a separate ops concern.

## Alternatives considered

- **Two-tier candidate pool → published set.** Cleanest separation, but doubles the
  model and the admin UI, and nothing shows publicly until an admin promotes —
  breaking "auto-generated by default." Rejected.
- **Trigram-frequency generation (no LLM).** Free and deterministic, but surfaces
  raw, messy user text and can't emit the agency tag. Rejected; curation can't
  fully rescue ugly prompts.
- **Auto-retiring seeds.** Self-cleaning once real questions arrive, but adds a
  hidden state transition and an edge case when usage later drops. Rejected in
  favor of seeds persisting until an admin removes them (predictable).

## Consequences

- One table, one migration; the admin page and public endpoint share it.
- The `/api/v1/public/` chokepoint exemption is now load-bearing for every current
  and future public GET (also covers `public_status`). Keep public routers strictly
  read-only under that prefix.
- Auto-generation quietly produces nothing until real usage crosses the threshold;
  that is intended, but means the feature "looks static" early in a deployment.
